chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – The Rubies and the Emeralds

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْيَوَاقِيتِ فَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahma Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Muhammad Bin Al Fazeyl, from Abu Al Hassan, from his father, from his grandfather who said,
‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘You should be wearing a ring with the rubies and these negate the poverty’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl from abu al-Hassan from his father from his grandfather who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, ‘You must use rubies as rings; they disallow poverty.’”
